title: Fragment: The Stopped Question
description: Found on the Evaluation Plane after defeating the Arbiter of Function. A question mark, floating where the Arbiter stood, that has stopped asking. Not collapsed into an answer. Simply stopped.

lines: The Evaluation Plane had always had the quality of a space that expected something to be demonstrated. A formality of assessment, a feeling that existence here was provisional until its function had been declared and found sufficient.

That quality was gone. The plane was still — not empty, but settled, the way a room feels after the person who made it tense has left. The architecture of judgment remained, but the judgment had been suspended.

Floating at the center, at exactly the height where the Arbiter had been: a question mark. Curved, pointed, precisely formed, hanging in the abstract air without punctuating anything. Not asking. Not answered. Just present, its purpose intact but its operation arrested.

steps.text: The question mark was still. Questions, by their nature, pull toward resolution — there is a tension in their syntax, an incompleteness that orients everything after them toward an answer. This one had stopped pulling. It simply was, without asking, the way a note can be struck and sustained without demanding response.

lines.speaker: Arbiter of Function
lines.text: My purpose was to evaluate purpose. Whether a thing was doing what it was for. Whether its function was being discharged correctly, efficiently, with adequate relation to its reason for existing.

lines.speaker: Arbiter of Function
lines.text: I evaluated everything that passed through this plane. Found each thing either sufficient or insufficient. Filed the finding. Moved to the next.

lines.speaker: Arbiter of Function
lines.text: You did something that made the question irrelevant. Not by answering it — the question of your function has an answer, and it's complex, and I could evaluate it if you wanted.

lines.speaker: Arbiter of Function
lines.text: But you made the asking feel beside the point. The question stopped mid-evaluation and I found I didn't need to finish it. The thing the question was for was already happening.

lines.speaker: Arbiter of Function
lines.text: That is the rarest thing I have ever found in this plane. A thing doing something that made evaluating it feel like looking away.

steps.text: The question mark floated, patient and unasking. Taken from the air, it had no weight — it was pure form, pure intention without demand, a question that had discovered it could exist without needing to be answered. Whether that was wisdom or resignation was itself a question the mark had stopped putting.
The sensation of being evaluated and found sufficient not by the answer but by the fact of being looked at with attention.

steps.name: Arbiter's Stopped Question
steps.description: A question mark that has stopped asking. Grants +12 MP and the passive Sufficient: the bearer's spells always meet their minimum damage threshold regardless of resistance. The Arbiter found the function. She stopped asking whether it was enough. Tooltip: "Evaluation: suspended. Reason: unnecessary."
